channel.title: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: Online University Throws a Lifeline to Afghan Women Shut Out of Education
item.description: Ever since childhood, Khatera’s (not her real name) dream was to study medicine at university and become a doctor. “Every time I saw doctors in their white coats, I would tell myself that I wished one day I could wear a similar coat and serve the people”, she recallls. Over the years, she felt that […]

item.title: The Cape Water Performance-Based Bond: A New Alliance for Cape Town’s Water Future
item.description: In 2018, Cape Town came perilously close to becoming the first major city in the world to run out of water. Known as “Day Zero”, it was more than just a crisis, it marked a pivotal moment. It made clear that water insecurity is not a distant threat, but an immediate reality. It also revealed […]

item.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 16 April: All eyes on mooted Israel-Lebanon talks
item.description: Amid reports of high-level talks later today between Israel and Lebanon, intense Israeli military strikes continued in southern Lebanese territory, including around the Hezbollah stronghold of Bint Jbeil. Hezbollah rocket fire continued into northern Israel also. In other developments, the US military announced on Wednesday that it had prevented 10 ships from navigating through the Strait of Hormuz, in line with its blockade of Iranian ports. Meanwhile, the Iranian military reportedly pledged to extend disruption to shipping to the Red Sea. Stay with us for live updates on this and UN agencies. App users can follow coverage here.

item.title: UN peacekeeping missions under strain as funding cuts and new threats grow
item.description: From election support in the Central African Republic to patrols in the disputed Abyei region, UN peacekeepers are operating in increasingly volatile environments – but shrinking resources and new threats, including drone warfare, are testing their ability to keep communities safe.

item.title: The Epstein files: Rights experts demand accountability, call for probe into trafficking allegations
item.description: UN independent human rights experts called on Thursday for justice and accountability for young women and girls who were trafficked systematically as part of allegations contained in the so-called Epstein files.

item.title: Deadly Russian strikes on Ukrainians ‘simply trying to live their lives’ must stop, says senior UN official
item.description: The latest wave of Russian attacks on Ukrainian civilians in Dnipro, Kyiv and Odesa, which killed and injured scores of civilians, was roundly condemned by Matthias Schmale, the UN Humanitarian Coordinator in the country.

item.title: New food alert platform helps humanitarians combat hunger
item.description: The UN World Food Programme (WFP) released its next-generation platform on Thursday known as HungerMap Live, a digital monitoring and intelligence site that integrates food security data with predictive modelling to help fight hunger in more than 50 countries.

item.title: Explainer: How the GEF Funds Global Environmental Action
item.description: The Global Environment Facility, widely known as the GEF, plays a central role in financing environmental protection across the world. It supports developing countries in tackling climate change, biodiversity loss, land degradation, pollution, and threats to ecosystems. Since its establishment in the early 1990s, the GEF has grown as a multilateral environmental fund, supporting projects […]

item.title: Shipping Industry Seeks Certainty as Experts Back Strong Net-Zero Framework
item.description: As global shipping braces for another round of high-stakes negotiations, a volatile mix of rising fuel costs, geopolitical tensions and deep political divisions is testing the fragile consensus around a proposed Net-Zero Framework (NZF) aimed at decarbonising one of the world’s most polluting industries. The talks, convened under the International Maritime Organization (IMO), come at […]

item.title: Wars Impose Lasting Economic Costs, While More Defense Spending Means Hard Choices
item.description: War is again defining the global landscape. After decades of relative calm following the Cold War, the number of active conflicts has surged in recent years to levels not seen since the end of the Second World War. Meanwhile, rising geopolitical tensions and heightened security concerns are prompting many governments to reassess their priorities and […]
